dbt Global Circuit Series: Dublin

Hosted by Ernesto O. and mike.burke

Dublin dbt Meetup
4.7
22 reviews
Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

We‚Äôll kick things off with a look inside how Squarespace uses dbt to power its analytics workflows, then dive into the latest tools and ideas we‚Äôve been shipping ‚ÄĒ what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers ‚ÄĒ folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what‚Äôs next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you‚Äôre curious about what‚Äôs new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions ‚ÄĒ you‚Äôre in the right place.

ūü§ĚOrganizer: dbt Labs (Mike Burke, Ernesto Ongaro)
ūüŹ†Venue Host: dbt Labs office
ūüćēCatering: refreshments will be provided

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

ūüďĚAgenda

5:30 - 6:00 | Check in/Registration
6:00 - 6:05 | Welcome Remarks by Mike Burke
6:05 - 6:35 | "How Squarespace leverages dbt" by Shahid Asmar (Staff Software Engineer @ Squarespace)
6:35 - 7:05 | ‚ÄúFor Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs‚ÄĚ by Ernesto Ongaro
7:05 - 8:00 | Closing Remarks by Mike Burke and Reception/Networking

‚ě°ÔłŹ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
ūü§ĚFor the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-dublin channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 50,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt‚Äôs analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Dublin dbt Meetup
4.7
22 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE ūüĎČ https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

ūüöÄ Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We‚Äôre hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.
